summary: Kenya's Inua Jamii programme provides cash transfers to orphans and vulnerable children, older persons, and persons with severe disabilities. The programme uses a digital single registry and increasingly leverages mobile money (M-Pesa) for payment delivery. The Enhanced Single Registry integrates beneficiary data across social protection programs. Digital registration is expanding but coverage remains incomplete in remote and arid areas.

sources.title: Inua Jamii - Ministry of Labour and Social Protection
World Bank - Kenya Social Protection Overview

notes: Inua Jamii reaches approximately 1.2 million beneficiaries across three cash transfer programmes. Mobile money payments via M-Pesa have significantly improved delivery efficiency. The National Safety Net Program (NSNP) supported by the World Bank continues to strengthen digital systems, but targeting and enrollment in arid and semi-arid counties remain challenging.
